race.name: Tour de Fukushima
race.tagline: UCI Gran Fondo qualifier through Fukushima's recovery zones

vitals.location_badge: Naraha Town, Tomioka Town, Fukushima, Japan

terrain.surface: Moderate with rolling short steep hills and sustained climbs along Pacific coastline and rugged inland highlands
features: Moderate with rolling short steep hills and sustained climbs along Pacific coastline and rugged inland highlands

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

course_description.character: Two-day format with **time trial**, **granfondo (140km)**, and **mediofondo (80km)** options through Hamadori coastal region. Routes traverse Pacific coastline, rugged inland highlands, and recently de-lifted evacuation zones (Namie/Odaka areas). **Suffering zones**: sustained climbs in coastal headwinds through disaster-affected terrain.

logistics.transport: Fukushima Airport (FKS) ~1hr from Hamadori region. Registration opened Feb 1, 2026 via fukushima-cycle-series.jp. Two-day event format TBD.

history.origin_story: Launched 2023 as Tool de Fukushima (fourth edition 2026). Part of Fukushima Revival Cycle Series promoting recovery 12+ years after 2011 Great East Japan Earthquake and nuclear disaster. Became UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier for 2026 Niseko Worlds.

biased_opinion.summary: Unique blend of competitive UCI gran fondo racing with profound cultural recovery narrative through recently decontaminated evacuation zones. Must-do for Japan-based riders seeking Niseko Worlds qualification.

final_verdict.one_liner: UCI Gran Fondo qualifier through Fukushima's recovery zones
